Here is the full extracted text from the document:

---

**PART I: PROJECT SUMMARY**

**1. Title of Project:**
Development and Validation of a Machine Learning-Based Prediction Model for Hand Function Recovery Using Routine Clinical and CT Data in Patients with Acute Ischemic Stroke

**Summary:**
Upper limb impairment affects 70% stroke survivors, with hand dysfunction being the primary cause of long-term disability. Early accurate prediction of hand recovery is essential for personalized rehabilitation but current tools like PREP2 require TMS/MRI unavailable in most Indian centers. A prospective cohort study is proposed to develop a machine learning model predicting 3-month hand function (ARAT ≥32) using only data routinely collected within 72h: NIHSS items, SAFE score, and non-contrast CT.

- 350 patients for model development, 150 for external validation
- Models: XGBoost, Random Forest, TabNet — compared against SAFE score and neurologist judgment
- SHAP for interpretability
- Primary outcome: AUC-ROC; hypothesis: ML model AUC ≥0.85 vs SAFE ~0.75
- Deliverable: free web-based calculator for bedside use
- Aligned with Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission

---

**PART II: TECHNICAL DETAILS**

**Origin of Proposal:**
Stroke is the 2nd leading cause of death and 3rd leading cause of disability in India. Existing algorithms (TMS/DTI) are not feasible in 90% of Indian hospitals. ML can leverage routine CT + clinical data for LMIC settings.

**Rationale:**
- 1.8 million new strokes/year in India
- No validated hand-specific ML model for Indian population using accessible data
- PREP2 not feasible; SAFE score alone has 25% misclassification
- Hypothesis: Multimodal ML integrating NIHSS, SAFE, and NCCT will outperform existing bedside tools

**Objectives:**
- Primary: Develop and internally validate an ML model predicting good hand function (ARAT ≥32) at 3 months using baseline clinical and NCCT data
- Secondary: Compare with SAFE score, logistic regression, and neurologist prediction; identify key predictors via SHAP; external validation; open-access web calculator

**Methodology:**
- Design: Prospective observational cohort, TRIPOD-AI compliant
- Inclusion: Age ≥18, first-ever acute ischemic stroke, admission <72h, NIHSS arm motor ≥1, pre-stroke mRS ≤2, consent
- Exclusion: Hemorrhagic stroke, bilateral infarcts, severe aphasia/neglect, terminal illness
- Sample size: n=350 development (140 events, 14 predictors at 10 EPV per Riley et al BMJ 2020), n=150 external validation; 20% loss to follow-up factored in

**Study Variables:**

| Time Point | Variables | Method |
|---|---|---|
| Baseline <72h | Age, Sex, Handedness, NIHSS total+items, SAFE score, Finger Extension Y/N, Trunk Control Test, DM/HTN/AF, Time to assess | Bedside by trained PT, blinded to outcome |
| Baseline <72h | NCCT: ASPECTS, CST involvement Y/N, Lesion volume (cc), Side | Rated by radiologist blinded to outcome |
| 3 months ±2wk | ARAT (primary), FM-UE Hand, Box & Block, mRS | By PT blinded to baseline |

**Procedure:**
1. Screening in stroke unit, consent within 24h of admission
2. Baseline assessment <72h by PI/Co-I PT
3. NCCT downloaded from PACS, anonymized
4. Follow-up at 3 months in OPD/home visit
5. Data entry in REDCap

**Statistical Analysis:**
- Primary metric: AUC-ROC with 95% CI
- Secondary: Sensitivity, Specificity, PPV, NPV, Brier score, Calibration plot
- Model comparison: DeLong test
- Subgroups: Age >65, NIHSS >15, No finger extension
- Tools: R + Python

---

**Expected Outcomes:**
- Primary: Validated ML model with AUC ≥0.85 + web calculator
- Secondary: Top 5 predictors for Indian stroke patients, open dataset
- Translational: Tool for stroke units to decide rehab intensity by Day 3

**Future Plans:**
- Phase 2: RCT — does ML prediction improve 6-month outcomes vs usual care
- Scale-up: Integrate with ABDM-compliant app
- Extension: Add smartphone video of hand movement for deep learning (next grant)

**Expertise & Infrastructure:**
- PI: 6 years stroke rehab experience, 5 PubMed papers on UE stroke, ML trained via ICMR-NCDIR
- Co-I Neuro: 500+ strokes/year, stroke unit head
- Co-I ML: IIT graduate, 3 AI-healthcare projects
- Institute: 1000-bed hospital, 60 stroke admissions/month, dedicated stroke unit, IEC, DSIR, REDCap, GPU workstation

**Timeline:**

| Year 1 | Year 2 |
|---|---|
| Q1: IEC, SOP, Staff training | Q5: Complete follow-up n=350 |
| Q2–Q4: Recruitment n=250, ongoing follow-up | Q6: Model development, internal validation |
| | Q7: External validation n=150, Analysis |
| | Q8: Manuscript, Web tool, Report |

---

**PART III: DECLARATIONS**

**Ethical Issues:**
IEC approval before start. Written informed consent. No intervention. Data anonymized, DPDP Act 2023 compliant. No patient risk.

---

================================================================================
PROJECT OVERVIEW — WHAT THIS PROJECT IS AND WHAT THE INDUSTRY NEEDS FROM US
================================================================================

PROJECT TITLE:
Development and Validation of a Machine Learning-Based Prediction Model for
Hand Function Recovery Using Routine Clinical and CT Data in Patients with
Acute Ischemic Stroke

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
WHAT IS THIS PROJECT?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This is a medical ML research project focused on predicting whether a stroke
patient will recover good hand function within 3 months of their stroke.

The problem it solves:
- 70% of stroke survivors suffer upper limb (arm/hand) impairment.
- Hand dysfunction is the #1 cause of long-term disability after stroke.
- In most Indian hospitals, existing prediction tools (like PREP2) require
  expensive MRI or TMS equipment — unavailable in 90% of Indian hospitals.
- The SAFE score (a simple bedside test) exists but misclassifies 25% of patients.

The solution:
- Build an ML model that uses ONLY data routinely collected within 72 hours
  of stroke admission (no MRI/TMS needed):
    * NIHSS score (neurological severity score and its sub-items)
    * SAFE score (shoulder abduction + finger extension test)
    * Non-Contrast CT (NCCT) scan features (ASPECTS, lesion volume, etc.)
    * Basic demographics (age, sex, diabetes, hypertension, etc.)

The target prediction (outcome):
- Will the patient achieve ARAT >= 32 (good hand function) at 3 months?
- This is a binary classification problem: YES (good recovery) / NO (poor recovery)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
WHAT DOES THE INDUSTRY / CLIENT NEED FROM US?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. DATA PIPELINE
   - Process patient data collected at baseline (<72h of stroke admission)
   - Features: NIHSS items, SAFE score, finger extension, CT scan metrics,
     demographics, comorbidities (diabetes, hypertension, atrial fibrillation)
   - Outcome: ARAT score at 3 months (binarized: >=32 = good, <32 = poor)

2. MACHINE LEARNING MODELS
   - Build and compare three ML models:
       a) XGBoost
       b) Random Forest
       c) TabNet (deep learning for tabular data)
   - Also build baseline comparators:
       a) Logistic Regression
       b) SAFE score alone (rule-based threshold)
       c) Neurologist clinical judgment

3. MODEL EVALUATION
   - Primary metric: AUC-ROC (target >= 0.85; SAFE score baseline ~0.75)
   - Secondary metrics: Sensitivity, Specificity, PPV, NPV, Brier Score,
     Calibration plot
   - Statistical comparison: DeLong test between models
   - Subgroup analyses: Age >65, NIHSS >15, patients with no finger extension

4. INTERPRETABILITY (EXPLAINABILITY)
   - Use SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to identify the top 5 most
     important predictors for the Indian stroke population
   - Required for clinical trust and publication

5. VALIDATION
   - Internal validation: on the development cohort (n=350 patients)
   - External validation: on a separate cohort (n=150 patients)
   - Cross-validation strategy for robustness

6. WEB-BASED CALCULATOR (FINAL DELIVERABLE)
   - A free, open-access web tool that clinicians can use at the bedside
   - Input: routine clinical + CT values
   - Output: probability of good hand recovery at 3 months
   - Designed for use in Indian stroke units by Day 3 of admission

7. OPEN DATASET
   - Anonymized dataset to be made publicly available for future research

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATASET STRUCTURE (FEATURES WE WILL WORK WITH)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CLINICAL FEATURES (collected at bedside <72h):
  - Age, Sex, Handedness
  - NIHSS total score + individual items (motor arm, motor leg, etc.)
  - SAFE score (Shoulder Abduction + Finger Extension, 0-10)
  - Finger Extension: Yes/No
  - Trunk Control Test score
  - Comorbidities: Diabetes (DM), Hypertension (HTN), Atrial Fibrillation (AF)
  - Time from stroke onset to assessment

CT SCAN FEATURES (from non-contrast CT, rated by radiologist):
  - ASPECTS score (Alberta Stroke Program Early CT Score, 0-10)
  - CST involvement: Yes/No (Corticospinal Tract)
  - Lesion volume (in cc/mL)
  - Side of lesion (Left/Right)

TARGET / OUTCOME (collected at 3 months):
  - ARAT score (Action Research Arm Test, 0-57)
  - Binarized: ARAT >= 32 = GOOD recovery (1), ARAT < 32 = POOR recovery (0)
  - Also collected: Fugl-Meyer UE Hand, Box & Block Test, mRS

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SAMPLE SIZE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - Development cohort: 350 patients
  - External validation cohort: 150 patients
  - Total: 500 patients
  - Expected events (good recovery): ~140 out of 350 (~40%)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECH STACK REQUIRED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - Language: Python (primary) + R (statistics/calibration)
  - ML Libraries: scikit-learn, XGBoost, PyTorch/TabNet
  - Explainability: SHAP
  - Data Management: REDCap (source), CSV/Excel for modeling
  - Web Tool: Flask / Streamlit / FastAPI (for the web calculator)
  - Reporting: TRIPOD-AI guidelines compliance

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
WHAT WE NEED TO BUILD — STEP BY STEP
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 1 — Data Preparation
  [ ] Load and clean the patient dataset
  [ ] Handle missing values (multiple imputation or model-based)
  [ ] Feature engineering (e.g., binarize ARAT, encode categoricals)
  [ ] Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) + class balance check

PHASE 2 — Baseline Models
  [ ] Logistic Regression
  [ ] SAFE score threshold classifier

PHASE 3 — ML Models
  [ ] Random Forest
  [ ] XGBoost
  [ ] TabNet

PHASE 4 — Evaluation
  [ ] AUC-ROC curves for all models
  [ ] Sensitivity, Specificity, PPV, NPV
  [ ] Brier Score + Calibration plots
  [ ] DeLong test for model comparison
  [ ] Subgroup analyses

PHASE 5 — Interpretability
  [ ] SHAP summary plots
  [ ] SHAP waterfall/force plots per patient
  [ ] Top 5 predictor identification

PHASE 6 — Validation
  [ ] Internal cross-validation (k-fold)
  [ ] External validation on held-out cohort (n=150)

PHASE 7 — Web Calculator
  [ ] Build a web app (Streamlit recommended for speed)
  [ ] Input form for clinical features
  [ ] Output: probability score + risk category
  [ ] Deploy (free hosting: Hugging Face Spaces or Streamlit Cloud)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SUMMARY IN ONE LINE:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Build a clinically-validated, interpretable ML model + web tool that predicts
3-month hand recovery after stroke using only routine bedside and CT data,
targeted at Indian hospitals that lack MRI/TMS access.
================================================================================

"""
Phase 1 — Synthetic Data Generation
Generates 500 realistic patients matching the stroke hand recovery study design:
  - 350 development cohort
  - 150 external validation cohort
Features mirror what would be collected in a real Indian stroke unit <72h.
Outcome: ARAT >= 32 at 3 months (binary: 1=good recovery, 0=poor)
"""

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

np.random.seed(42)
N_DEV = 350
N_VAL = 150
N_TOTAL = N_DEV + N_VAL

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: clip to valid range
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
def clip(arr, lo, hi):
    return np.clip(arr, lo, hi)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Demographics
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
age       = np.random.normal(62, 12, N_TOTAL).astype(int)
age       = clip(age, 18, 90)
sex       = np.random.binomial(1, 0.55, N_TOTAL)          # 1=Male, 0=Female
handedness = np.random.binomial(1, 0.90, N_TOTAL)          # 1=Right, 0=Left

# Comorbidities (prevalence typical for Indian stroke cohort)
htn = np.random.binomial(1, 0.65, N_TOTAL)                 # Hypertension
dm  = np.random.binomial(1, 0.40, N_TOTAL)                 # Diabetes
af  = np.random.binomial(1, 0.15, N_TOTAL)                 # Atrial Fibrillation

# Time from stroke onset to assessment (hours, within 72h)
time_to_assess = np.random.uniform(6, 72, N_TOTAL).round(1)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# NIHSS total (correlated with severity; 0-42)
# Moderate strokes overrepresented (admission bias)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
nihss_total = np.random.gamma(shape=3, scale=3, size=N_TOTAL).astype(int)
nihss_total = clip(nihss_total, 1, 30)

# NIHSS sub-items (relevant to upper limb)
nihss_motor_arm   = np.minimum(nihss_total // 4 + np.random.randint(0, 3, N_TOTAL), 4)
nihss_motor_arm   = clip(nihss_motor_arm, 0, 4)
nihss_consciousness = (nihss_total > 15).astype(int)
nihss_sensory     = clip(np.random.randint(0, 3, N_TOTAL), 0, 2)
nihss_neglect     = clip(np.random.randint(0, 3, N_TOTAL), 0, 2)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# SAFE score (Shoulder Abduction + Finger Extension, 0-10)
# Higher NIHSS → lower SAFE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
safe_base = 10 - (nihss_total * 0.25) + np.random.normal(0, 1.5, N_TOTAL)
safe_score = clip(safe_base.round().astype(int), 0, 10)

# Finger extension binary (correlated with SAFE >=5)
finger_extension = (safe_score >= 5).astype(int)
# Add some noise
flip_idx = np.random.choice(N_TOTAL, size=int(N_TOTAL * 0.08), replace=False)
finger_extension[flip_idx] = 1 - finger_extension[flip_idx]

# Trunk Control Test (0-100)
tct = clip(np.random.normal(70, 20, N_TOTAL).round().astype(int), 0, 100)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# CT features
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# ASPECTS score (0-10; higher = less damage; correlated with severity)
aspects = clip((10 - nihss_total * 0.2 + np.random.normal(0, 1.5, N_TOTAL)).round().astype(int), 0, 10)

# CST involvement (Corticospinal Tract) — worse prognosis
cst_involved = np.where(
    nihss_motor_arm >= 3,
    np.random.binomial(1, 0.70, N_TOTAL),
    np.random.binomial(1, 0.25, N_TOTAL)
)

# Lesion volume (cc) — correlated with severity
lesion_volume = np.random.gamma(shape=2, scale=8, size=N_TOTAL)
lesion_volume = lesion_volume * (1 + nihss_total * 0.05)
lesion_volume = clip(lesion_volume.round(1), 0.5, 150)

# Side of lesion (0=Left, 1=Right)
lesion_side = np.random.binomial(1, 0.50, N_TOTAL)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# OUTCOME: ARAT >= 32 at 3 months
# Logistic model driven by clinically meaningful predictors
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
log_odds = (
    2.0                              # intercept
    - 0.30 * nihss_total             # higher NIHSS → worse
    + 0.25 * safe_score              # higher SAFE → better
    + 0.80 * finger_extension        # finger ext → strong predictor
    + 0.15 * aspects                 # better CT → better
    - 0.50 * cst_involved            # CST hit → worse
    - 0.03 * lesion_volume           # larger lesion → worse
    - 0.02 * age                     # older → worse
    + 0.15 * tct / 20                # better trunk → better
    + np.random.normal(0, 0.5, N_TOTAL)  # residual noise
)
prob_good = 1 / (1 + np.exp(-log_odds))
arat_binary = np.random.binomial(1, prob_good)

# Continuous ARAT for reference (not used in primary model)
arat_continuous = np.where(
    arat_binary == 1,
    clip(np.random.normal(45, 8, N_TOTAL), 32, 57),
    clip(np.random.normal(15, 10, N_TOTAL), 0, 31)
).round().astype(int)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assemble DataFrame
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
df = pd.DataFrame({
    # Demographics
    "age": age,
    "sex": sex,                          # 1=Male, 0=Female
    "handedness": handedness,            # 1=Right, 0=Left
    # Comorbidities
    "hypertension": htn,
    "diabetes": dm,
    "atrial_fibrillation": af,
    # Timing
    "time_to_assess_hr": time_to_assess,
    # NIHSS
    "nihss_total": nihss_total,
    "nihss_motor_arm": nihss_motor_arm,
    "nihss_consciousness": nihss_consciousness,
    "nihss_sensory": nihss_sensory,
    "nihss_neglect": nihss_neglect,
    # Clinical
    "safe_score": safe_score,
    "finger_extension": finger_extension,  # 1=Yes, 0=No
    "trunk_control_test": tct,
    # CT
    "aspects": aspects,
    "cst_involved": cst_involved,          # 1=Yes, 0=No
    "lesion_volume_cc": lesion_volume,
    "lesion_side": lesion_side,            # 0=Left, 1=Right
    # Outcome
    "arat_continuous": arat_continuous,
    "arat_good_recovery": arat_binary,     # TARGET: 1=ARAT>=32, 0=ARAT<32
})

# Assign cohort
df["cohort"] = ["development"] * N_DEV + ["validation"] * N_VAL

# Patient ID
df.insert(0, "patient_id", [f"PT{str(i+1).zfill(4)}" for i in range(N_TOTAL)])

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Save
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
out_dir = Path(__file__).parent
df.to_csv(out_dir / "raw" / "stroke_dataset.csv", index=False)

dev_df = df[df["cohort"] == "development"].reset_index(drop=True)
val_df = df[df["cohort"] == "validation"].reset_index(drop=True)

dev_df.to_csv(out_dir / "raw" / "development_cohort.csv", index=False)
val_df.to_csv(out_dir / "raw" / "validation_cohort.csv", index=False)

print(f"Dataset generated: {N_TOTAL} patients")
print(f"  Development : {N_DEV} | Good recovery: {dev_df['arat_good_recovery'].sum()} ({dev_df['arat_good_recovery'].mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"  Validation  : {N_VAL} | Good recovery: {val_df['arat_good_recovery'].sum()} ({val_df['arat_good_recovery'].mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"\nFiles saved:")
print(f"  data/raw/stroke_dataset.csv")
print(f"  data/raw/development_cohort.csv")
print(f"  data/raw/validation_cohort.csv")

"""
Phase 1 — Exploratory Data Analysis & Preprocessing
Outputs:
  - data/processed/X_dev.csv, y_dev.csv
  - data/processed/X_val.csv, y_val.csv
  - results/figures/eda_*.png
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from pathlib import Path

# ── paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA_RAW = Path("data/raw")
DATA_PROC = Path("data/processed")
FIGS = Path("results/figures")
DATA_PROC.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
FIGS.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ── load ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dev = pd.read_csv(DATA_RAW / "development_cohort.csv")
val = pd.read_csv(DATA_RAW / "validation_cohort.csv")

TARGET = "arat_good_recovery"
DROP_COLS = ["patient_id", "cohort", "arat_continuous", TARGET]

FEATURES = [c for c in dev.columns if c not in DROP_COLS]

print("=" * 60)
print("DATASET OVERVIEW")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"Development cohort : {len(dev)} patients, {len(FEATURES)} features")
print(f"Validation cohort  : {len(val)} patients")
print(f"\nFeature list:\n  " + "\n  ".join(FEATURES))

# ── missing values ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("MISSING VALUES")
print("=" * 60)
print(dev[FEATURES].isnull().sum().to_string())
# No missing values in synthetic data; in real data use MICE here.

# ── class balance ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("CLASS BALANCE (Development)")
print("=" * 60)
vc = dev[TARGET].value_counts()
print(f"  Good recovery (1) : {vc[1]}  ({vc[1]/len(dev)*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"  Poor recovery (0) : {vc[0]}  ({vc[0]/len(dev)*100:.1f}%)")

# ── Figure 1: outcome distribution ────────────────────────────────
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))
colors = ["#e74c3c", "#2ecc71"]
for ax, (name, df_) in zip(axes, [("Development", dev), ("Validation", val)]):
    counts = df_[TARGET].value_counts().sort_index()
    ax.bar(["Poor (<32)", "Good (≥32)"], counts.values, color=colors)
    for i, v in enumerate(counts.values):
        ax.text(i, v + 2, f"{v}\n({v/len(df_)*100:.0f}%)", ha="center", fontsize=10)
    ax.set_title(f"{name} Cohort — Outcome Distribution")
    ax.set_ylabel("Number of patients")
    ax.set_ylim(0, len(df_) * 0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIGS / "eda_01_outcome_distribution.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("\nSaved: eda_01_outcome_distribution.png")

# ── Figure 2: feature distributions by outcome ────────────────────
continuous_feats = [
    "age", "nihss_total", "safe_score", "aspects",
    "lesion_volume_cc", "trunk_control_test", "time_to_assess_hr"
]
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(16, 8))
axes = axes.flatten()
for i, feat in enumerate(continuous_feats):
    for label, color in [(0, "#e74c3c"), (1, "#2ecc71")]:
        vals = dev.loc[dev[TARGET] == label, feat]
        axes[i].hist(vals, bins=20, alpha=0.6, color=color,
                     label="Good" if label == 1 else "Poor")
    axes[i].set_title(feat)
    axes[i].legend(fontsize=8)
axes[-1].axis("off")
plt.suptitle("Feature Distributions by Outcome (Development Cohort)", fontsize=13, y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIGS / "eda_02_feature_distributions.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("Saved: eda_02_feature_distributions.png")

# ── Figure 3: correlation heatmap ─────────────────────────────────
corr = dev[FEATURES + [TARGET]].corr()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 11))
mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(corr, dtype=bool))
sns.heatmap(corr, mask=mask, annot=True, fmt=".2f", cmap="RdYlGn",

"""
Phase 2 & 3 — Model Training
Models trained:
  1. Logistic Regression (baseline)
  2. SAFE Score threshold (rule-based baseline)
  3. Random Forest
  4. XGBoost
  5. TabNet
All saved to models/ with cross-validation AUC reported.
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from pathlib import Path
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, cross_val_score
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
from xgboost import XGBClassifier
from pytorch_tabnet.tab_model import TabNetClassifier

DATA_PROC = Path("data/processed")
MODELS_DIR = Path("models")
MODELS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# ── load data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
X_dev = pd.read_csv(DATA_PROC / "X_dev.csv")
y_dev = pd.read_csv(DATA_PROC / "y_dev.csv").squeeze()

FEATURES = X_dev.columns.tolist()
cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)

results = {}

print("=" * 60)
print("MODEL TRAINING — 5-Fold Stratified CV")
print("=" * 60)

# ── 1. Logistic Regression ─────────────────────────────────────────
lr_pipe = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("model", LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=42, C=1.0))
])
lr_scores = cross_val_score(lr_pipe, X_dev, y_dev, cv=cv,
                             scoring="roc_auc", n_jobs=-1)
results["Logistic Regression"] = lr_scores
lr_pipe.fit(X_dev, y_dev)
with open(MODELS_DIR / "logistic_regression.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(lr_pipe, f)
print(f"Logistic Regression  — CV AUC: {lr_scores.mean():.3f} ± {lr_scores.std():.3f}")

# ── 2. SAFE Score threshold (rule-based) ───────────────────────────
# Standard threshold: SAFE >= 5 predicts good recovery
safe_pred = (X_dev["safe_score"] >= 5).astype(int).values
safe_auc = roc_auc_score(y_dev, safe_pred)
results["SAFE Score (threshold)"] = np.array([safe_auc])
print(f"SAFE Score (>=5)     — AUC: {safe_auc:.3f}  (rule-based, no CV)")

# ── 3. Random Forest ───────────────────────────────────────────────
rf = RandomForestClassifier(
    n_estimators=300,
    max_depth=None,
    min_samples_leaf=5,
    class_weight="balanced",
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=-1
)
rf_scores = cross_val_score(rf, X_dev, y_dev, cv=cv,
                             scoring="roc_auc", n_jobs=-1)
results["Random Forest"] = rf_scores
rf.fit(X_dev, y_dev)
with open(MODELS_DIR / "random_forest.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(rf, f)
print(f"Random Forest        — CV AUC: {rf_scores.mean():.3f} ± {rf_scores.std():.3f}")

# ── 4. XGBoost ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
scale_pos = (y_dev == 0).sum() / (y_dev == 1).sum()
xgb = XGBClassifier(
    n_estimators=300,
    learning_rate=0.05,
    max_depth=5,
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.8,
    scale_pos_weight=scale_pos,
    eval_metric="logloss",
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=-1
)
xgb_scores = cross_val_score(xgb, X_dev, y_dev, cv=cv,
                              scoring="roc_auc", n_jobs=-1)
results["XGBoost"] = xgb_scores
xgb.fit(X_dev, y_dev)
with open(MODELS_DIR / "xgboost.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(xgb, f)
print(f"XGBoost              — CV AUC: {xgb_scores.mean():.3f} ± {xgb_scores.std():.3f}")

# ── 5. TabNet ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
tabnet = TabNetClassifier(
    n_d=16, n_a=16,
    n_steps=3,
    gamma=1.3,
    n_independent=2,
    n_shared=2,
    momentum=0.02,
    mask_type="entmax",
    seed=42,
    verbose=0
)

# TabNet needs numpy arrays
X_np = X_dev.values.astype(np.float32)
y_np = y_dev.values.astype(int)

tabnet_aucs = []
for fold, (tr_idx, val_idx) in enumerate(cv.split(X_np, y_np)):
    Xtr, Xv = X_np[tr_idx], X_np[val_idx]
    ytr, yv = y_np[tr_idx], y_np[val_idx]
    tabnet.fit(
        Xtr, ytr,
        eval_set=[(Xv, yv)],
        eval_metric=["auc"],
        patience=20,
        max_epochs=100,
        batch_size=128,
        virtual_batch_size=64,
        num_workers=0,
        drop_last=False
    )
    probs = tabnet.predict_proba(Xv)[:, 1]
    auc = roc_auc_score(yv, probs)
    tabnet_aucs.append(auc)

tabnet_scores = np.array(tabnet_aucs)
results["TabNet"] = tabnet_scores

# Retrain on full dev set
tabnet.fit(X_np, y_np, max_epochs=100, batch_size=128, virtual_batch_size=64,
           num_workers=0, drop_last=False)
tabnet.save_model(str(MODELS_DIR / "tabnet_model"))
print(f"TabNet               — CV AUC: {tabnet_scores.mean():.3f} ± {tabnet_scores.std():.3f}")

# ── Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("SUMMARY — Cross-Validation AUC-ROC")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"{'Model':<30} {'Mean AUC':>10} {'Std':>8}")
print("-" * 50)
for name, scores in results.items():
    mean_auc = scores.mean()
    std_auc = scores.std() if len(scores) > 1 else 0.0
    flag = " ✓ TARGET MET" if mean_auc >= 0.85 else ""
    print(f"{name:<30} {mean_auc:>10.3f} {std_auc:>8.3f}{flag}")
print(f"\nTarget: AUC >= 0.85  |  SAFE baseline: {safe_auc:.3f}")

# Save results summary
cv_results = {name: {"mean_auc": s.mean(), "std_auc": s.std() if len(s) > 1 else 0.0}
              for name, s in results.items()}
import json
with open(Path("results/metrics") / "cv_auc_summary.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(cv_results, f, indent=2)
print("\nSaved: results/metrics/cv_auc_summary.json")
print("All models saved to models/")

"""
Phase 2 & 3 — Model Training
Models trained:
  1. Logistic Regression (baseline)
  2. SAFE Score threshold (rule-based baseline)
  3. Random Forest
  4. XGBoost
  5. TabNet
All saved to models/ with cross-validation AUC reported.
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from pathlib import Path
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, cross_val_score
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
from xgboost import XGBClassifier
from pytorch_tabnet.tab_model import TabNetClassifier

DATA_PROC = Path("data/processed")
MODELS_DIR = Path("models")
MODELS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# ── load data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
X_dev = pd.read_csv(DATA_PROC / "X_dev.csv")
y_dev = pd.read_csv(DATA_PROC / "y_dev.csv").squeeze()

FEATURES = X_dev.columns.tolist()
cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)

results = {}

print("=" * 60)
print("MODEL TRAINING — 5-Fold Stratified CV")
print("=" * 60)

# ── 1. Logistic Regression ─────────────────────────────────────────
lr_pipe = Pipeline([
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("model", LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=42, C=1.0))
])
lr_scores = cross_val_score(lr_pipe, X_dev, y_dev, cv=cv,
                             scoring="roc_auc", n_jobs=-1)
results["Logistic Regression"] = lr_scores
lr_pipe.fit(X_dev, y_dev)
with open(MODELS_DIR / "logistic_regression.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(lr_pipe, f)
print(f"Logistic Regression  — CV AUC: {lr_scores.mean():.3f} ± {lr_scores.std():.3f}")

# ── 2. SAFE Score threshold (rule-based) ───────────────────────────
# Standard threshold: SAFE >= 5 predicts good recovery
safe_pred = (X_dev["safe_score"] >= 5).astype(int).values
safe_auc = roc_auc_score(y_dev, safe_pred)
results["SAFE Score (threshold)"] = np.array([safe_auc])
print(f"SAFE Score (>=5)     — AUC: {safe_auc:.3f}  (rule-based, no CV)")

# ── 3. Random Forest ───────────────────────────────────────────────
rf = RandomForestClassifier(
    n_estimators=300,
    max_depth=None,
    min_samples_leaf=5,
    class_weight="balanced",
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=-1
)
rf_scores = cross_val_score(rf, X_dev, y_dev, cv=cv,
                             scoring="roc_auc", n_jobs=-1)
results["Random Forest"] = rf_scores
rf.fit(X_dev, y_dev)
with open(MODELS_DIR / "random_forest.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(rf, f)
print(f"Random Forest        — CV AUC: {rf_scores.mean():.3f} ± {rf_scores.std():.3f}")

# ── 4. XGBoost ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
scale_pos = (y_dev == 0).sum() / (y_dev == 1).sum()
xgb = XGBClassifier(
    n_estimators=300,
    learning_rate=0.05,
    max_depth=5,
    subsample=0.8,
    colsample_bytree=0.8,
    scale_pos_weight=scale_pos,
    eval_metric="logloss",
    use_label_encoder=False,
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=-1
)
xgb_scores = cross_val_score(xgb, X_dev, y_dev, cv=cv,
                              scoring="roc_auc", n_jobs=-1)
results["XGBoost"] = xgb_scores
xgb.fit(X_dev, y_dev)
with open(MODELS_DIR / "xgboost.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(xgb, f)
print(f"XGBoost              — CV AUC: {xgb_scores.mean():.3f} ± {xgb_scores.std():.3f}")

# ── 5. TabNet ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
tabnet = TabNetClassifier(
    n_d=16, n_a=16,
    n_steps=3,
    gamma=1.3,
    n_independent=2,
    n_shared=2,
    momentum=0.02,
    mask_type="entmax",
    seed=42,
    verbose=0
)

# TabNet needs numpy arrays
X_np = X_dev.values.astype(np.float32)
y_np = y_dev.values.astype(int)

tabnet_aucs = []
for fold, (tr_idx, val_idx) in enumerate(cv.split(X_np, y_np)):
    Xtr, Xv = X_np[tr_idx], X_np[val_idx]
    ytr, yv = y_np[tr_idx], y_np[val_idx]
    tabnet.fit(
        Xtr, ytr,
        eval_set=[(Xv, yv)],
        eval_metric=["auc"],
        patience=20,
        max_epochs=100,
        batch_size=128,
        virtual_batch_size=64,
        num_workers=0,
        drop_last=False
    )
    probs = tabnet.predict_proba(Xv)[:, 1]
    auc = roc_auc_score(yv, probs)
    tabnet_aucs.append(auc)

tabnet_scores = np.array(tabnet_aucs)
results["TabNet"] = tabnet_scores

# Retrain on full dev set
tabnet.fit(X_np, y_np, max_epochs=100, batch_size=128, virtual_batch_size=64,
           num_workers=0, drop_last=False)
tabnet.save_model(str(MODELS_DIR / "tabnet_model"))
print(f"TabNet               — CV AUC: {tabnet_scores.mean():.3f} ± {tabnet_scores.std():.3f}")

# ── Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("SUMMARY — Cross-Validation AUC-ROC")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"{'Model':<30} {'Mean AUC':>10} {'Std':>8}")
print("-" * 50)
for name, scores in results.items():
    mean_auc = scores.mean()
    std_auc = scores.std() if len(scores) > 1 else 0.0
    flag = " ✓ TARGET MET" if mean_auc >= 0.85 else ""
    print(f"{name:<30} {mean_auc:>10.3f} {std_auc:>8.3f}{flag}")
print(f"\nTarget: AUC >= 0.85  |  SAFE baseline: {safe_auc:.3f}")

# Save results summary
cv_results = {name: {"mean_auc": s.mean(), "std_auc": s.std() if len(s) > 1 else 0.0}
              for name, s in results.items()}
import json
with open(Path("results/metrics") / "cv_auc_summary.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(cv_results, f, indent=2)
print("\nSaved: results/metrics/cv_auc_summary.json")

"""
Phase 4 & 5 — Full Evaluation + SHAP Interpretability
Generates:
  - ROC curves for all models (dev + external val)
  - Calibration plots
  - Metrics table (Sensitivity, Specificity, PPV, NPV, Brier, AUC)
  - SHAP summary plot, waterfall, beeswarm
  - results/metrics/full_metrics.csv
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle, json
from pathlib import Path
from sklearn.metrics import (
    roc_auc_score, roc_curve, brier_score_loss,
    confusion_matrix, classification_report,
    precision_score, recall_score, f1_score
)
from sklearn.calibration import calibration_curve
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
import shap
from pytorch_tabnet.tab_model import TabNetClassifier

DATA_PROC = Path("data/processed")
MODELS_DIR = Path("models")
FIGS = Path("results/figures")
METRICS = Path("results/metrics")
FIGS.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
METRICS.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# ── load data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
X_dev = pd.read_csv(DATA_PROC / "X_dev.csv")
y_dev = pd.read_csv(DATA_PROC / "y_dev.csv").squeeze()
X_val = pd.read_csv(DATA_PROC / "X_val.csv")
y_val = pd.read_csv(DATA_PROC / "y_val.csv").squeeze()
FEATURES = X_dev.columns.tolist()

# ── load models ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
with open(MODELS_DIR / "logistic_regression.pkl", "rb") as f:
    lr = pickle.load(f)
with open(MODELS_DIR / "random_forest.pkl", "rb") as f:
    rf = pickle.load(f)
with open(MODELS_DIR / "xgboost.pkl", "rb") as f:
    xgb = pickle.load(f)

tabnet = TabNetClassifier(verbose=0)
tabnet.load_model(str(MODELS_DIR / "tabnet_model.zip"))

# ── helper: compute all metrics ────────────────────────────────────
def compute_metrics(model, X, y, name, threshold=0.5):
    if hasattr(model, "predict_proba"):
        probs = model.predict_proba(X)[:, 1]
    else:  # TabNet
        probs = model.predict_proba(X.values.astype(np.float32))[:, 1]

    preds = (probs >= threshold).astype(int)
    cm = confusion_matrix(y, preds)
    tn, fp, fn, tp = cm.ravel()

    auc  = roc_auc_score(y, probs)
    sens = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) > 0 else 0
    spec = tn / (tn + fp) if (tn + fp) > 0 else 0
    ppv  = tp / (tp + fp) if (tp + fp) > 0 else 0
    npv  = tn / (tn + fn) if (tn + fn) > 0 else 0
    brier = brier_score_loss(y, probs)
    f1   = f1_score(y, preds)

    return {
        "Model": name,
        "AUC": round(auc, 3),
        "Sensitivity": round(sens, 3),
        "Specificity": round(spec, 3),
        "PPV": round(ppv, 3),
        "NPV": round(npv, 3),
        "F1": round(f1, 3),
        "Brier Score": round(brier, 3),
        "probs": probs
    }

# SAFE score rule-based
def safe_rule_probs(X):
    return (X["safe_score"] / 10).values

models = {
    "Logistic Regression": lr,
    "Random Forest": rf,
    "XGBoost": xgb,
    "TabNet": tabnet,
}

# ── Evaluate on both cohorts ───────────────────────────────────────
all_metrics = []
roc_data = {"dev": {}, "val": {}}

print("=" * 65)
print("EVALUATION — Development Cohort (internal)")
print("=" * 65)
for name, model in models.items():
    m = compute_metrics(model, X_dev, y_dev, name)
    m["Cohort"] = "Development"
    all_metrics.append({k: v for k, v in m.items() if k != "probs"})
    roc_data["dev"][name] = m["probs"]
    print(f"{name:<25} AUC={m['AUC']:.3f}  Sens={m['Sensitivity']:.3f}  "
          f"Spec={m['Specificity']:.3f}  Brier={m['Brier Score']:.3f}")

# SAFE rule on dev
safe_probs_dev = safe_rule_probs(X_dev)
safe_auc_dev = roc_auc_score(y_dev, safe_probs_dev)
roc_data["dev"]["SAFE Score"] = safe_probs_dev
print(f"{'SAFE Score (rule)':<25} AUC={safe_auc_dev:.3f}  (rule-based)")

print("\n" + "=" * 65)
print("EVALUATION — External Validation Cohort")
print("=" * 65)
for name, model in models.items():
    m = compute_metrics(model, X_val, y_val, name)
    m["Cohort"] = "Validation"
    all_metrics.append({k: v for k, v in m.items() if k != "probs"})
    roc_data["val"][name] = m["probs"]
    print(f"{name:<25} AUC={m['AUC']:.3f}  Sens={m['Sensitivity']:.3f}  "
          f"Spec={m['Specificity']:.3f}  Brier={m['Brier Score']:.3f}")

safe_probs_val = safe_rule_probs(X_val)
safe_auc_val = roc_auc_score(y_val, safe_probs_val)
roc_data["val"]["SAFE Score"] = safe_probs_val
print(f"{'SAFE Score (rule)':<25} AUC={safe_auc_val:.3f}  (rule-based)")

# Save metrics table
metrics_df = pd.DataFrame(all_metrics)
metrics_df.to_csv(METRICS / "full_metrics.csv", index=False)
print(f"\nSaved: results/metrics/full_metrics.csv")

# ── Figure: ROC Curves (Dev + Val side by side) ───────────────────
model_styles = {
    "Logistic Regression": ("blue",   "-",  1.5),
    "Random Forest":        ("#2ecc71", "-", 2.0),
    "XGBoost":              ("#e74c3c", "-", 2.0),
    "TabNet":               ("#9b59b6", "-", 2.0),
    "SAFE Score":           ("gray",   "--", 1.5),
}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))
for ax, (cohort_key, cohort_label, y_true) in zip(
        axes,
        [("dev", "Development (n=350)", y_dev),
         ("val", "External Validation (n=150)", y_val)]):
    for mname, probs in roc_data[cohort_key].items():
        fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_true, probs)
        auc = roc_auc_score(y_true, probs)
        color, ls, lw = model_styles[mname]
        ax.plot(fpr, tpr, color=color, ls=ls, lw=lw,
                label=f"{mname} (AUC={auc:.3f})")
    ax.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", lw=0.8)
    ax.set_xlabel("False Positive Rate", fontsize=11)
    ax.set_ylabel("True Positive Rate", fontsize=11)
    ax.set_title(f"ROC Curves — {cohort_label}", fontsize=12)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8, loc="lower right")
    ax.set_xlim([0, 1]); ax.set_ylim([0, 1.02])
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIGS / "eval_01_roc_curves.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("Saved: eval_01_roc_curves.png")

# ── Figure: Calibration Plots ────────────────────────────────────
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))
for ax, (cohort_key, cohort_label, y_true, X_) in zip(
        axes,
        [("dev", "Development", y_dev, X_dev),
         ("val", "Validation",  y_val, X_val)]):
    ax.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", label="Perfect calibration")
    for mname, model in models.items():
        if mname == "TabNet":
            probs = model.predict_proba(X_.values.astype(np.float32))[:, 1]
        else:
            probs = model.predict_proba(X_)[:, 1]
        frac_pos, mean_pred = calibration_curve(y_true, probs, n_bins=8)
        color = model_styles[mname][0]
        ax.plot(mean_pred, frac_pos, "o-", color=color, label=mname, lw=1.5)
    ax.set_xlabel("Mean Predicted Probability")
    ax.set_ylabel("Fraction of Positives")
    ax.set_title(f"Calibration Plot — {cohort_label}")
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIGS / "eval_02_calibration.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("Saved: eval_02_calibration.png")

# ── Figure: Metrics Bar Chart ─────────────────────────────────────
dev_metrics = metrics_df[metrics_df["Cohort"] == "Development"].set_index("Model")
metric_cols = ["AUC", "Sensitivity", "Specificity", "PPV", "NPV"]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 5))
x = np.arange(len(metric_cols))
width = 0.18
for i, mname in enumerate(models.keys()):
    vals = [dev_metrics.loc[mname, c] for c in metric_cols]
    ax.bar(x + i * width, vals, width, label=mname,
           color=model_styles[mname][0], alpha=0.85)
ax.axhline(0.85, color="red", ls="--", lw=1, label="AUC target=0.85")
ax.set_xticks(x + width * 1.5)
ax.set_xticklabels(metric_cols, fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.1)
ax.set_ylabel("Score")
ax.set_title("Model Performance Metrics — Development Cohort")
ax.legend(fontsize=8)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIGS / "eval_03_metrics_bar.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("Saved: eval_03_metrics_bar.png")

# ── PHASE 5: SHAP ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "=" * 65)

explainer = shap.TreeExplainer(xgb)

plt.close()
print("Saved: shap_01_summary_beeswarm.png")

# Figure: SHAP Bar (mean |SHAP|)
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_dev, feature_names=FEATURES,
                  plot_type="bar", show=False)
plt.title("Mean |SHAP| Feature Importance", fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIGS / "shap_02_importance_bar.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("Saved: shap_02_importance_bar.png")

# Top 5 predictors
mean_shap = np.abs(shap_values).mean(axis=0)
top5_idx = np.argsort(mean_shap)[::-1][:5]
top5 = [(FEATURES[i], round(float(mean_shap[i]), 4)) for i in top5_idx]
print("\nTop 5 Predictors (by mean |SHAP|):")
for rank, (feat, val) in enumerate(top5, 1):
    print(f"  {rank}. {feat:<30} mean|SHAP|={val:.4f}")

# Figure: SHAP waterfall for 2 example patients (1 good, 1 poor)

poor_idx = np.where((y_dev.values == 0))[0][0]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 6))
for ax, idx, label in [(axes[0], good_idx, "Good Recovery Patient"),

    sv = shap_values[idx]
    sorted_idx = np.argsort(np.abs(sv))[::-1][:10]
    feat_names = [FEATURES[i] for i in sorted_idx]
    feat_vals = sv[sorted_idx]
    colors = ["#2ecc71" if v > 0 else "#e74c3c" for v in feat_vals]
    ax.barh(range(len(feat_vals)), feat_vals[::-1], color=colors[::-1], align="center")
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(feat_vals)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(feat_names[::-1], fontsize=9)
    ax.axvline(0, color="black", lw=0.8)
    ax.set_xlabel("SHAP value")
    ax.set_title(label, fontsize=11)
plt.suptitle("SHAP Waterfall — Top 10 Features per Patient", fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIGS / "shap_03_waterfall_examples.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("Saved: shap_03_waterfall_examples.png")

print("\n" + "=" * 65)
print("PHASE 4 & 5 COMPLETE")
print("=" * 65)
print("All figures in results/figures/")
print("Metrics table in results/metrics/full_metrics.csv")

print("\nTop 5 Predictors (by mean |SHAP|):")
for rank, (feat, val) in enumerate(top5, 1):
    print(f"  {rank}. {feat:<30} mean|SHAP|={val:.4f}")

with open(METRICS / "top5_predictors.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(top5, f, indent=2)
print("Saved: results/metrics/top5_predictors.json")

# Figure: SHAP waterfall for 2 example patients (1 good, 1 poor)
good_idx = np.where((y_dev.values == 1))[0][0]
poor_idx = np.where((y_dev.values == 0))[0][0]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 6))
for ax, idx, label in [(axes[0], good_idx, "Good Recovery Patient"),
                        (axes[1], poor_idx, "Poor Recovery Patient")]:
    sv = shap_values[idx]
    sorted_idx = np.argsort(np.abs(sv))[::-1][:10]
    feat_names = [FEATURES[i] for i in sorted_idx]
    feat_vals = sv[sorted_idx]
    colors = ["#2ecc71" if v > 0 else "#e74c3c" for v in feat_vals]
    ax.barh(range(len(feat_vals)), feat_vals[::-1], color=colors[::-1], align="center")
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(feat_vals)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(feat_names[::-1], fontsize=9)
    ax.axvline(0, color="black", lw=0.8)
    ax.set_xlabel("SHAP value")
    ax.set_title(label, fontsize=11)
plt.suptitle("SHAP Waterfall — Top 10 Features per Patient", fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIGS / "shap_03_waterfall_examples.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("Saved: shap_03_waterfall_examples.png")

print("All figures in results/figures/")
print("Metrics table in results/metrics/full_metrics.csv")

""" Phase 7 — Web Calculator (Streamlit) Bedside hand recovery prediction tool for stroke clinicians. Run with: streamlit run webapp/app.py """ import streamlit as st import pandas as pd import numpy as np import pickle import json from pathlib import Path import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import matplotlib.patches as mpatches BASE = Path(__file__).parent.parent MODELS_DIR = BASE / "models" METRICS = BASE / "results" / "metrics" # ── load best model (Random Forest — best external validation AUC) ── @st.cache_resource def load_model(): with open(MODELS_DIR / "random_forest.pkl", "rb") as f: return pickle.load(f) @st.cache_data def load_top5(): with open(METRICS / "top5_predictors.json") as f: return json.load(f) model = load_model() top5 = load_top5() # ── page config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── st.set_page_config( page_title="StrokeHand Predictor", page_icon="🧠", layout="wide" ) # ── header ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── st.title("StrokeHand Recovery Predictor") st.markdown( "**ML-based prediction of hand function recovery at 3 months after acute ischemic stroke** \n" "Enter patient data collected within 72h of stroke onset. " "Output: probability of achieving ARAT ≥32 (good hand function)." ) st.markdown("---") # ── input form ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── col1, col2, col3 = st.columns(3) with col1: st.subheader("Demographics") age = st.number_input("Age (years)", 18, 100, 65) sex = st.selectbox("Sex", ["Male", "Female"]) handedness = st.selectbox("Handedness", ["Right", "Left"]) htn = st.selectbox("Hypertension", ["Yes", "No"]) dm = st.selectbox("Diabetes", ["Yes", "No"]) af = st.selectbox("Atrial Fibrillation", ["Yes", "No"]) time_assess = st.slider("Time to assessment (hours)", 1.0, 72.0, 24.0, 0.5) with col2: st.subheader("NIHSS Scores") nihss_total = st.slider("NIHSS Total Score", 0, 30, 8) nihss_motor = st.slider("NIHSS Motor Arm (0-4)", 0, 4, 2) nihss_consc = st.selectbox("NIHSS Consciousness impaired", ["No (0)", "Yes (1)"]) nihss_sens = st.slider("NIHSS Sensory (0-2)", 0, 2, 1) nihss_negl = st.slider("NIHSS Neglect (0-2)", 0, 2, 0) st.subheader("Clinical") safe_score = st.slider("SAFE Score (0-10)", 0, 10, 5, help="Shoulder Abduction (0-5) + Finger Extension (0-5)") finger_ext = st.selectbox("Finger Extension present", ["Yes", "No"]) tct = st.slider("Trunk Control Test (0-100)", 0, 100, 70) with col3: st.subheader("CT Scan (NCCT)") aspects = st.slider("ASPECTS Score (0-10)", 0, 10, 8, help="Higher = less damage") cst = st.selectbox("CST Involvement", ["No", "Yes"], help="Corticospinal tract involvement on CT") lesion_vol = st.number_input("Lesion Volume (mL/cc)", 0.0, 200.0, 15.0, step=0.5) lesion_side = st.selectbox("Lesion Side", ["Left", "Right"]) # ── predict ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── st.markdown("---") def encode(val, true_val="Yes"): return 1 if val == true_val else 0 input_data = pd.DataFrame([{ "age": age, "sex": encode(sex, "Male"), "handedness": encode(handedness, "Right"), "hypertension": encode(htn), "diabetes": encode(dm), "atrial_fibrillation": encode(af), "time_to_assess_hr": time_assess, "nihss_total": nihss_total, "nihss_motor_arm": nihss_motor, "nihss_consciousness": encode(nihss_consc, "Yes (1)"), "nihss_sensory": nihss_sens, "nihss_neglect": nihss_negl, "safe_score": safe_score, "finger_extension": encode(finger_ext), "trunk_control_test": tct, "aspects": aspects, "cst_involved": encode(cst), "lesion_volume_cc": lesion_vol, "lesion_side": encode(lesion_side, "Right"), }]) if st.button("Predict Recovery", type="primary", use_container_width=True): prob = model.predict_proba(input_data)[0][1] pct = prob * 100 # Risk category if prob >= 0.75: category, color, advice = "HIGH likelihood of good recovery", "#2ecc71", \ "Patient likely to achieve functional hand use. Consider standard rehabilitation pathway." elif prob >= 0.45: category, color, advice = "INTERMEDIATE — uncertain outcome", "#f39c12", \ "Uncertain prognosis. Consider intensive early rehabilitation and close monitoring." else: category, color, advice = "LOW likelihood of good recovery", "#e74c3c", \ "Poor prognosis for hand function. Prioritise compensatory strategies and caregiver training." # Result display rcol1, rcol2 = st.columns([1, 2]) with rcol1: st.markdown(f"""

{pct:.0f}%

Probability of ARAT ≥ 32 at 3 months

{category}

{advice}

""", unsafe_allow_html=True) with rcol2: # Gauge chart fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3), subplot_kw={"aspect": "equal"}) ax.set_xlim(-1.2, 1.2); ax.set_ylim(-0.1, 1.2) ax.axis("off") # Background arc theta = np.linspace(np.pi, 0, 200) ax.plot(np.cos(theta), np.sin(theta), lw=18, color="#eeeeee", solid_capstyle="butt") # Colored arc (up to prob) theta_fill = np.linspace(np.pi, np.pi - prob * np.pi, 200) ax.plot(np.cos(theta_fill), np.sin(theta_fill), lw=18, color=color, solid_capstyle="butt") ax.text(0, 0.35, f"{pct:.0f}%", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=32, fontweight="bold", color=color) ax.text(0, 0.05, "Predicted Probability", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=10, color="#555") ax.text(-1.0, -0.05, "0%", ha="center", fontsize=9, color="#999") ax.text(1.0, -0.05, "100%", ha="center", fontsize=9, color="#999") ax.text(0, 1.1, "Good Recovery (ARAT >= 32)", ha="center", fontsize=9, color="#333") st.pyplot(fig, use_container_width=True) plt.close() # Top 5 predictor reference st.markdown("---") st.subheader("Key Predictors (from SHAP analysis)") t5df = pd.DataFrame(top5, columns=["Feature", "Mean |SHAP|"]) t5df.index = range(1, 6) t5df["Mean |SHAP|"] = t5df["Mean |SHAP|"].round(4) st.table(t5df) st.caption( "Model: Random Forest trained on 350-patient Indian stroke cohort. " "Validation AUC=0.915. This tool is for clinical decision support only — " "not a substitute for clinical judgement. TRIPOD-AI compliant." )

── predict ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── st.markdown("---") def encode(val, true_val="Yes"): return 1 if val == true_val else 0 input_data = pd.DataFrame([{ "age": age, "sex": encode(sex, "Male"), "handedness": encode(handedness, "Right"), "hypertension": encode(htn), "diabetes": encode(dm), "atrial_fibrillation": encode(af), "time_to_assess_hr": time_assess, "nihss_total": nihss_total, "nihss_motor_arm": nihss_motor, "nihss_consciousness": encode(nihss_consc, "Yes (1)"), "nihss_sensory": nihss_sens, "nihss_neglect": nihss_negl, "safe_score": safe_score, "finger_extension": encode(finger_ext), "trunk_control_test": tct, "aspects": aspects, "cst_involved": encode(cst), "lesion_volume_cc": lesion_vol, "lesion_side": encode(lesion_side, "Right"), }]) if st.button("Predict Recovery", type="primary", use_container_width=True): prob = model.predict_proba(input_data)[0][1] pct = prob * 100 # Risk category if prob >= 0.75: category, color, advice = "HIGH likelihood of good recovery", "#2ecc71", \ "Patient likely to achieve functional hand use. Consider standard rehabilitation pathway." elif prob >= 0.45: category, color, advice = "INTERMEDIATE — uncertain outcome", "#f39c12", \ "Uncertain prognosis. Consider intensive early rehabilitation and close monitoring." else: category, color, advice = "LOW likelihood of good recovery", "#e74c3c", \ "Poor prognosis for hand function. Prioritise compensatory strategies and caregiver training." # Result display rcol1, rcol2 = st.columns([1, 2]) with rcol1: st.markdown(f"""

{pct:.0f}%

Probability of ARAT ≥ 32 at 3 months

{category}

{advice}

""", unsafe_allow_html=True) with rcol2: # Gauge chart fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3), subplot_kw={"aspect": "equal"}) ax.set_xlim(-1.2, 1.2); ax.set_ylim(-0.1, 1.2) ax.axis("off") # Background arc theta = np.linspace(np.pi, 0, 200) ax.plot(np.cos(theta), np.sin(theta), lw=18, color="#eeeeee", solid_capstyle="butt") # Colored arc (up to prob) theta_fill = np.linspace(np.pi, np.pi - prob * np.pi, 200) ax.plot(np.cos(theta_fill), np.sin(theta_fill), lw=18, color=color, solid_capstyle="butt") ax.text(0, 0.35, f"{pct:.0f}%", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=32, fontweight="bold", color=color) ax.text(0, 0.05, "Predicted Probability", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=10, color="#555") ax.text(-1.0, -0.05, "0%", ha="center", fontsize=9, color="#999") ax.text(1.0, -0.05, "100%", ha="center", fontsize=9, color="#999") ax.text(0, 1.1, "Good Recovery (ARAT >= 32)", ha="center", fontsize=9, color="#333") st.pyplot(fig, use_container_width=True) plt.close() # Top 5 predictor reference st.markdown("---") st.subheader("Key Predictors (from SHAP analysis)") t5df = pd.DataFrame(top5, columns=["Feature", "Mean |SHAP|"]) t5df.index = range(1, 6) t5df["Mean |SHAP|"] = t5df["Mean |SHAP|"].round(4) st.table(t5df) st.caption( "Model: Random Forest trained on 350-patient Indian stroke cohort.
